meta.instructions: Edit this file to add, update or remove contacts. Then commit and push to GitHub. Netlify will auto-deploy in under 60 seconds and both your phone and computer will be updated.

contacts.name: Bradley Mogere
contacts.met: Existing business relationship — co-founder of supply venture
contacts.why: Medical supply chain. Operations partner. Revenue source. Core trust anchor.
contacts.know: Long-standing relationship. Know his working style, motivations, and reliability track record.
contacts.knows: Hospital procurement officers, medical wholesalers Nairobi, distribution contacts
contacts.favours: Equal partners — mutual value. Owe: written partnership agreement by May 2026.
contacts.nextAction: Monthly call this week. Consignment count, margin review, partnership agreement discussion.

contacts.name: Melchizedek Anyonje
contacts.met: Existing business relationship — co-partner in supply venture
contacts.why: Supply chain partner. Operational role. Third partner in supply venture.
contacts.know: Active partner. Works well with Bradley. Logistics-focused approach.
contacts.favours: Equal partner. Owe: formalised written agreement, transparent financials.
contacts.nextAction: 3-way call with Bradley this month. Formalise written partnership agreement.

contacts.name: Sandrah
contacts.why: Life partner. Co-emigration planner. Fellow physician. B1/B2 visa applicant. Future mother of our children.
contacts.know: Everything. Petname: daddy shark. Graduating 2027. Diplomatic passport history. Extensive travel record.
contacts.knows: MTRH clinical network, her faculty contacts, family connections, diplomatic contacts
contacts.favours: She gives everything. Nails every 2 weeks. Monthly surprise. Weekly date night pre-booked. Always.
contacts.nextAction: Plan April getaway NOW — book before exam week. B1/B2 docs together this weekend.

contacts.name: Dr. Zachary Gitlin
contacts.role: US University — Attending Physician — MTRH Partnership
contacts.why: LOR potential. Clinical mentorship. Fellowship program intel. Research collaboration.
contacts.know: Internal medicine and paediatric specialist, very intelligent and knowledgable, and IU .
contacts.knows: Program directors at home institution, other AMPATH faculty
contacts.favours: Shared MTRH SHA claims process insight. Offer: digital systems tour, Uzimatek demo.
contacts.nextAction: Follow-up email by THURSDAY. Reference specific thing discussed at Fireside. Propose Zoom for April.

contacts.name: Dr. Josh Smith-Sreen
contacts.why: USMLE peer intel. IMG match experience. Program culture insider. Future co-author potential.
contacts.know: Grew up partly in Nairobi cause parents worked for USAID.
contacts.favours: Offer: Kenya clinical experience insight. Anki deck sharing if relevant.

contacts.name: Africa Health Collaborative
contacts.met: Mastercard Foundation Healthcare Innovation pitch — runner-up
contacts.why: Investor introductions. Grant pipeline. Media coverage for Uzimatek. Mastercard Foundation adjacent.
contacts.know: Warm reception post-pitch. Type their portfolio focus and key contacts here.
contacts.knows: Novastar Ventures, DRK Foundation, Mastercard Foundation team, East Africa health-tech ecosystem
contacts.favours: Pitched at their event — added credibility. Offer: quarterly Uzimatek updates, co-create with their team.
contacts.nextAction: EMAIL THIS WEEK. Q1 Uzimatek update. Ask for introduction to 1 investor.

contacts.name: Mastercard Foundation
contacts.why: Direct grant pipeline. Future funding. YouthAfrica Works network access. Endorsement credibility.
contacts.know: Runner-up — they believe in the concept. Type their programme area and pitch feedback here.
contacts.knows: Other Mastercard grantees, foundation investment arm, co-applicants
contacts.favours: Pitched at their event. Offer: monthly impact updates, co-create with programme team.
contacts.nextAction: April email: 6-month post-pitch update. Show traction — clinics signed, revenue growing.

contacts.name: MTRH Consultant
contacts.why: MSPE / Dean's letter for ERAS. Internal Uzimatek SHA pilot champion. Clinical reference.
contacts.know: Type what you know about this consultant from your rotations — specialty, personality, research interests.
contacts.knows: Hospital administration, MTRH procurement, MU faculty network
contacts.favours: Nothing formal yet. Offer: Uzimatek as a solution to their SHA claims headaches.
contacts.nextAction: Visit during next MTRH rotation. Position yourself as Uzimatek founder early.

contacts.name: Strathmore MBA Director
contacts.met: Not yet contacted — TARGET for Q4 2026
contacts.why: MBA application 2028. Africa business network. Faculty mentor access. East Africa investor connections.
contacts.know: Research their MBA programme specifics and faculty before reaching out.
contacts.knows: Nairobi private sector leaders, MBA alumni network, East Africa business community
contacts.favours: Nothing yet. Offer: Uzimatek as a live MBA case study.
contacts.nextAction: Email Q4 2026. Information session or campus visit Q1 2027.

contacts.name: MOH Policy Lead
contacts.role: Ministry of Health Kenya — Policy Division
contacts.met: Target via Africa Health Collaborative introduction
contacts.why: Healthcare policy network. SHA regulatory navigation. Government access for Uzimatek.
contacts.know: Add name and specific background when you have a contact name.
contacts.knows: Ministry of Health leadership, SHA leadership, hospital CEOs, county health directors
contacts.favours: Nothing yet. Build relationship through AHC introduction first.
contacts.nextAction: Request warm introduction via Africa Health Collaborative.

contacts.name: EU Health Tech Investor
contacts.role: Partech Africa / Leapfrog Investments — European Office
contacts.why: Pre-seed and seed stage African health-tech investment. European diaspora capital for Uzimatek.
contacts.know: Type investor thesis and portfolio companies when researched.
contacts.knows: Other EU Africa-focused investors, global health funders, DFI contacts
contacts.favours: Nothing yet. Offer: East Africa market thesis + Uzimatek pitch deck.
contacts.nextAction: LinkedIn outreach: lead with Mastercard Foundation runner-up + Uzimatek one-pager.

contacts.name: MU Faculty
contacts.role: Moi University School of Medicine — Clinical Faculty
contacts.met: Medical school — clinical faculty across rotations
contacts.why: Dean's letter / MSPE for ERAS application. Clinical reference letter. Internal academic ally.
contacts.know: Type their specialty and any personal connection points from your rotations.
contacts.knows: MU Faculty network, MTRH administration, East Africa medical education leadership
contacts.favours: Nothing formal yet. Offer: Uzimatek digital health research angle as collaboration.
contacts.nextAction: Office visit next rotation. Ask intelligent clinical questions. Be memorable.

name: Dr. Tony Nguyen
why: LOR potential for ECFMG/ERAS. Direct mentor in anaesthesiology — my current rotation specialty. Described OR safety culture as 'military-like'. Deep expertise in perioperative systems and closed-loop communication.
know: Very structured and systems-focused. Described KP's OR culture explicitly. Narrated EPIC EHR's real-time anaesthetic logging. Strong teacher energy.
knows: KP anaesthesiology department, OR safety faculty, perioperative medicine network
favours: He gave me 1 week of structured OR exposure. I owe him a meaningful update on my anaesthesia rotation progress at MTRH.
nextAction: Email referencing the 'military-like OR culture' insight — directly relevant to my current anaesthesia rotation. Update on MTRH comparison. Ask about observership possibility 2028.

name: Dr. Jennifer Nguyen
met: 2025 KP Rotation — Week 2 Diagnostic Cardiology / TEE
why: Cardiology is my target specialty. She supervised TEE observation — a modality unavailable at MTRH. Direct connection to the cardiology department I want to work in. LOR potential.
know: Supervised TEE procedure for valvular assessment and LAA visualisation. Calm, precise, excellent teacher. Connection to structural heart disease team.
knows: KP cardiology department, structural heart disease team, TAVR programme
favours: She gave me direct TEE exposure — a rare privilege for a medical student. I owe her a progress update and a note on how this shaped my cardiology ambitions.
nextAction: Email: reference the TEE session and how it contextualised TAVR. Express cardiology career interest. Ask about cardiology elective or observership 2028.

name: Dr. Leong
role: Interventional Cardiologist — Kaiser Permanente Cath Lab
met: 2025 KP Rotation — Week 3 Cardiac Catheterisation Laboratory
why: HIGHEST PRIORITY cardiology contact. Interventional cardiology is my target sub-specialty. He let me scrub in for a PCI — stood at the table for wire navigation and stent deployment. Embodied 'resident-as-teacher' culture. LOR from an interventional cardiologist = gold for cardiology fellowship.
know: Narrated every step during PCI. Invited questions throughout. Explained fluoroscopy anatomy in real time. Generous with access — let me scrub in, which is unusual for a visiting student.
knows: KP interventional cardiology team, cath lab network, structural heart disease programme (TAVR team)
favours: He let me scrub into a PCI. That is an extraordinary privilege for a visiting international student. I owe him a detailed, thoughtful thank-you update.
nextAction: EMAIL PRIORITY 1. Reference scrubbing into the PCI — specific wire navigation moment. Update on cardiology interest. Ask directly: would he support a 2028 interventional cardiology observership?

name: Dr. Howard
met: 2025 KP Rotation — Week 3 Cardiac Care Unit
why: Asked me to formally present a case (75yo AV block). That is significant — it means he assessed my clinical performance directly. LOR potential. CCU exposure highly relevant for IM residency and cardiology fellowship.
know: Discussed pharmacogenomics — hydralazine-nitrate combinations for African American patients with heart failure (BiDil trial data). Equity-conscious prescriber. Asked me to formally present. High standards.
knows: KP CCU team, inpatient cardiology network, heart failure programme
favours: He gave me the rare opportunity of a formal case presentation in the CCU. I owe him a substantive clinical update.
nextAction: Email referencing the AV block case presentation. Note how it deepened my interest in inpatient cardiology. Update on MTRH cardiology exposure.

name: Dr. Kohan
met: 2025 KP Rotation — Week 3 Cardiac Care Unit (alongside Dr. Howard)
why: Co-supervising attendant in CCU. Exposed me to complex inpatient cardiology — ACS, decompensated HF, complex arrhythmias, post-procedural monitoring. LOR potential as CCU co-attending.
know: Co-attending with Dr. Howard in the CCU. Part of the team that supervised my formal case presentation.
favours: Supervised my CCU rotation. I owe a professional thank-you and update.
nextAction: Email alongside Dr. Howard outreach. Brief, professional. Reference CCU experience and ongoing cardiology interest.

name: Dr. Doris
why: EP is a cardiology sub-specialty — directly relevant to fellowship pathway. Supervised WPW ablation and AF ablation using CARTO 3D electroanatomical mapping. Rare procedural exposure. LOR potential.
know: Ran an ECG interpretation clinic before the EP lab — systematic review of complex arrhythmias. Thorough teacher. Supervised two ablation procedures.
knows: KP electrophysiology department, arrhythmia network, CARTO mapping specialists
favours: Gave me EP lab access and ran a dedicated ECG teaching session. I owe a substantive clinical update.
nextAction: Email referencing the WPW ablation and CARTO mapping system. Express interest in EP as a possible subspecialty within cardiology fellowship.

name: Dr. Puente
role: Paediatrician — Kaiser Permanente Baldwins Hills Crenshaw / Inglewood
met: 2025 KP Rotation — Weeks 1 and 4 Paediatrics outpatient clinic
why: Saw me across two separate weeks — rare continuity. Modelled motivational interviewing for vaccine hesitancy and adolescent HEADSS framework. LOR potential given repeated exposure.
know: Handles ethnically diverse catchment area. Extremely patient with vaccine-hesitant families. Structured motivational interviewing — evidence-based communication style. Warm and approachable.
knows: KP paediatrics department, community health outreach network
favours: Welcomed me back for Week 4 — continuity is a mark of trust. I owe a warm personal thank-you.
nextAction: Personal email — reference the HEADSS framework and how I've started using it at MTRH. Warm, genuine update.

name: Dr. Caivano
role: Haematologist / Sickle Cell Specialist — Kaiser Permanente West Los Angeles
met: 2025 KP Rotation — Week 1 Sickle Cell Service
why: SCD is one of the most common haematological diagnoses at MTRH. Dr. Caivano's team showed me what comprehensive SCD management looks like (voxelotor, crizanlizumab, PCA pumps, multidisciplinary team). Huge relevance for Uzimatek data and global health research angle.
know: Runs a multidisciplinary SCD service. The gap between KP and MTRH SCD management was the starkest clinical contrast of the rotation. PCA pump approach, social worker integration.
knows: KP haematology department, SCD research network, global health medicine contacts
favours: Gave me SCD clinic access — deeply relevant to my MTRH experience. I owe a reflective update on the contrast.
nextAction: Email referencing the SCD life expectancy contrast (5 years in SSA vs 50+ years in KP). Mention Uzimatek angle — this data gap is what drives health informatics work in Africa.

name: Dr. Ethnasios
role: Internist — Kaiser Permanente Outpatient / Primary Care
met: 2025 KP Rotation — Weeks 2 and 4 Internal Medicine outpatient
why: Internal Medicine is my US residency pathway. He supervised IM outpatient across two weeks — direct assessment of my clinical skills. LOR potential. His communication approach (motivational interviewing, obesity counselling) was a specific learning point.
know: Warm, methodical teacher. Modelled evidence-based communication as a practised skill. Childhood obesity counselling and vaccine hesitancy handling observed across two sessions.
knows: KP internal medicine department, primary care network, outpatient medicine faculty
favours: Supervised IM outpatient across two separate weeks. I owe a clinical update and thank-you.
nextAction: Email: reference the IM outpatient sessions. Express that IM is my US residency target. Ask about his thoughts on IMG preparation for IM residency.

name: Dr. Parekh
why: Witnessed robotic partial nephrectomy and radical prostatectomy. Surgical innovation relevant to Uzimatek's health system narrative. LOR potential — unusual specialty but demonstrates range.
know: Operates DaVinci robotic system. 90% of cases managed as same-day or 23-hour procedures. EBL consistently under 100mL. Technically precise.
knows: KP urology department, robotic surgery programme
favours: Gave me OR access for robotic surgery. Professional thank-you owed.
nextAction: Brief professional email. Reference the DaVinci partial nephrectomy — the 7-degree wrist articulation was remarkable. Warm, concise.

name: Dr. Tammadon
met: 2025 KP Rotation — Week 2 Urological Surgery (DaVinci), alongside Dr. Parekh
why: Co-operating surgeon on the DaVinci robotic cases. LOR potential alongside Dr. Parekh.
know: Co-surgeon on robotic urology cases with Dr. Parekh.
knows: KP urology department, robotic surgery programme
favours: OR access during robotic surgery. Professional thank-you owed.
nextAction: Brief professional email alongside Dr. Parekh outreach.

name: Dr. Chi
met: 2025 KP Rotation — Week 4 Gender-Affirming Surgery (7-hour vaginoplasty)
why: One of the most surgically complex cases of the rotation — 7-hour penile inversion vaginoplasty. Demonstrated multidisciplinary surgical care. Unique exposure. LOR potential from a specialised surgical environment.
know: Performs complex gender-affirming surgery. Meticulous neurovascular dissection to preserve erogenous sensitivity. Patient journey spans 12-24 months from referral to post-op rehab. Highly specialised.
knows: KP plastic surgery department, gender-affirming surgery programme, multidisciplinary surgical teams
favours: Allowed observation of a 7-hour complex surgical case. Professional thank-you owed.
nextAction: Brief thoughtful email. Reference the multidisciplinary care pathway (psychiatry, endocrinology, physio, gynaecology) as a systems-thinking lesson.

name: Dr. Diego
why: Supervised UAE, G-tube placement, and port placement. IR is directly relevant to the Uzimatek data capture narrative (minimally invasive, high-value procedures). Unique exposure. LOR potential.
know: Performed UAE (Uterine Artery Embolisation), G-tube placement, and port placement. IR as an alternative to surgery — relevant to resource-limited settings discussion.
knows: KP interventional radiology department, IR network
favours: Gave me IR lab access. Professional thank-you owed.
nextAction: Email referencing UAE as a minimally invasive alternative to surgery. Note how IR's resource implications compare to MTRH — this is a Uzimatek-relevant insight.

name: Dr. Cara
met: 2025 KP Rotation — Week 5 Urgent Care (final day of rotation)
why: Full-spectrum urgent care exposure on the final day. Demonstrated KP's urgent care as a system pressure valve — preventing ED overcrowding. Relevant to MTRH design recommendations in electives report.
know: Final day of rotation. High-volume, fast-paced urgent care. Demonstrated how KP's urgent care reduces ED burden. Warm, efficient practitioner.
knows: KP urgent care network, emergency medicine contacts
favours: Gave me final-day clinical exposure. Brief professional thank-you owed.
nextAction: Brief thank-you email. Reference the urgent care as ED pressure valve insight — this influenced the MTRH recommendations in your electives report.
